chat.viewStepDetails: View step details in the task console

chatInput.enableLlmFirst: Enable an LLM in the configuration panel first.
chatInput.modelNotTested: This model has not passed a connection test
chatInput.modelNotTestedHint: This model has not passed a connection test. Run "Test connection" in the model configuration.
chatInput.modelBlocked: Model "{{name}}" has not passed a connection test. Open the model configuration and run "Test connection" before using it.
chatInput.datasourceBlocked: Data source "{{name}}" has not passed a connection test. Open the data source configuration and run "Test connection" before using it.

sessionConfig.lockedHelp: Data source and knowledge selections are locked after the first message in this chat.
sessionConfig.scopeHelp: Controls resources for this chat only. Manage global resources from Workspace Resources on the left.
sessionConfig.agentToolsScopeHelp: Controls MCP servers and Skills for this chat only.
sessionConfig.noConfigurationYet: No configuration yet. Add one from Workspace Resources on the left.

console.title: Task Console

empty.noStepSelectedDescription: Select a step from the center tool card or Trace timeline to inspect duration, action details, and output lineage.
empty.noStepsYetDescription: After you send a question, process steps appear here in order. Parallel tools are grouped under one step.
empty.noOutputsDescription: SQL, datasets, charts, and reports appear here after a question. Preview an item in a popup, or open its page to reference the whole or parts.

console.outputsHintExport: Preview any output in a popup, or open it on its own page to reference the whole or a selected part.
console.outputsHint: Preview any output in a popup, or open it on its own page.
console.outputsHintBackend: Preview and download require the backend artifact API.
console.previewTitle: Preview this output in a popup over the page
console.openPageTitle: Open its own page to reference a selected table region / text, or the whole artifact
console.seeOutputsTab: See the Outputs tab for the full list.
console.startPrompt: Send a question to start dataFoundry.
console.referenceWholeHint: Select a table region or text to reference just that part, or use “Reference whole” for the entire artifact.
console.noContentSelectedDescription: Select a visible output to inspect the full SQL, dataset, chart, or report.
console.noActionSelectedDescription: Select a data action to inspect its parameters, observations, and outputs.
console.noProcessStepSelectedDescription: Select a process step to inspect batch duration, child tools, and output lineage.
console.stepNoOutputsYet: This step has not directly produced outputs yet.
console.actionNoOutputs: This action did not directly produce outputs.
console.noChartDataDescription: The backend declared a chart artifact but has not reported points/series preview data yet.
console.exportFilteredResult: Export the current filtered / sorted result

assets.title: Assets
assets.description: Reusable workspace assets for future data tasks and @ mentions.
assets.panelHelp: Manage reusable workspace files for later @file selection or run_config.fileIds injection. These are separate from chat attachments.
assets.needSession: Open a chat session to enable workspace file upload.
assets.needSessionError: Select or open a chat session before uploading workspace files.
assets.needSessionTitle: Select or open a chat session before uploading
assets.deleteConfirm: Delete workspace file "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.
assets.empty: No reusable workspace files yet. Upload a file to add one.

configPanel.noToolsYet: No tools yet. Test the connection first to refresh the manifest.
configPanel.configuredDataSourcesHelp: Browse configured sources, sync schema, and open table-level previews.
configPanel.startFromGallery: Start from the adapter gallery to create one.
configPanel.uploadDocumentHelp: Upload a text document (.txt, .md, .csv, .tsv, .json, .yaml) or PDF (.pdf) for vectorization and retrieval.
configPanel.configuredKnowledgeBasesHelp: Upload documents to vectorize, browse indexed files, and edit retrieval settings.
configPanel.startKnowledgeBase: Create a knowledge base, then upload text or PDF files to vectorize.
configPanel.browseKnowledgeDocuments: Browse uploaded documents and upload more for vectorization
configPanel.knowledgeDocumentsHelp: Files uploaded to this knowledge base are chunked and indexed for retrieval.
configPanel.noKnowledgeDocumentsHelp: Upload a .txt or .pdf file to vectorize it into this knowledge base.
configPanel.configuredMcpServersHelp: Test connections to refresh tool manifests, and edit server settings.
configPanel.startMcpServer: Add an MCP server connection to expose tools to the agent.
configPanel.configuredSkillsHelp: Import Skill packages, validate semantics, and edit package settings.
configPanel.startSkill: Import a SKILL.md or .zip package to get started.
configPanel.configuredModelsHelp: Test model profiles and manage which LLM runs use by default.
configPanel.startModel: Add an LLM profile so data tasks can call a model.

descriptions.kb: Manage documents, indexes and retrieval settings for workspace knowledge.
descriptions.mcp: Manage MCP server connections. Tests refresh the cached tools manifest.
descriptions.skill: Import SKILL.md or .zip packages. Runtime payloads reference skill ids only.
descriptions.llm: Manage LLM model profiles. Runs reference activeLlmProfileId through run_config.

configPanel.availableByDefault: Available by default in the workspace
configPanel.notesDb: Data sources are registered through the REST API. Credentials are stored in secretRef and never returned in reads. Connection tests and schema sync update connectionStatus.
configPanel.notesKb: Knowledge documents and indexes are managed through the REST API. Runs retrieve by run_config.enabledKnowledgeIds.
configPanel.notesMcp: MCP servers are registered through the REST API. Connection tests refresh toolManifest and healthStatus.
configPanel.notesLlm: Model profiles are managed through the REST API. Runs switch with run_config.activeLlmProfileId.
configPanel.notesSkillBuiltin: Built-in Skills are provided by the server. Runs send only the skill id.
configPanel.notesSkillCustom: Custom Skills are uploaded through multipart REST. Package content is stored server-side.
configPanel.uploadSkillPackageHelp: Supports SKILL.md (with YAML frontmatter) or a .zip package. Create/replace uploads through REST API.
configPanel.skillNotesStructure: Standard structure: directory contains SKILL.md (YAML frontmatter + instructions)
configPanel.skillNotesContract: Backend contract: POST /api/v1/skills multipart upload; runs send only activeSkillId, not package content
configPanel.skillNotesBuiltin: Built-in Skills are provided by the server; the frontend only shows metadata
configPanel.skillNotesCustom: Custom Skills are uploaded through REST multipart; AG-UI context carries only the skill id
configPanel.mcpNotesRemote: Remote services commonly use SSE / Streamable HTTP URLs, for example https://host/mcp
configPanel.mcpNotesPersist: Configuration is persisted through POST /api/v1/mcp-servers; tokens are stored in secretRef
configPanel.mcpNotesRuns: Runs use run_config.enabledMcpServerIds to mount MCP tools
configPanel.llmNotesEnv: Server env: LLM_PROVIDER, LLM_BASE_URL, LLM_API_KEY, LLM_MODEL
configPanel.llmNotesPersist: Model profiles are managed through POST /api/v1/model-profiles; API keys are stored in secretRef
configPanel.llmNotesBuiltin: The Server default item is read-only and maps to LLM configuration from server env
configPanel.noToolsManifest: No tools yet. Test the connection first to refresh the manifest.
configPanel.testProbeUnavailable: Connectivity probe is not available for this resource type.
configPanel.providerConfigMissing: Model provider configuration is incomplete for "{{profile}}". Check the API key, base URL, and model name.
configPanel.mcpConfigInvalid: MCP server configuration is incomplete. Check the URL/command and transport.
configPanel.mcpStdioRequired: MCP stdio transport requires a command.
configPanel.revisionConflict: Configuration was updated while testing. Refresh and try again.
configPanel.resourceDeleted: This configuration was deleted while testing. Refresh and try again.

welcome.title: Data Analysis Workbench
welcome.subtitle: Ask in natural language. The agent can inspect schemas, run read-only SQL, and show the full trace in the console.
welcome.footerBeforeAt: Ask directly in the input below, or use
welcome.inspectSchemaDesc: List tables and fields for the active datasource
welcome.inspectSchemaPrompt: Show the tables in the datasource and summarize the main fields for each table
welcome.runSqlDesc: Generate and run read-only SQL from natural language
welcome.runSqlPrompt: Query total orders in the last 30 days, grouped by date
welcome.analyzeTrendsPrompt: Analyze monthly sales trends and identify the months with the largest swings

schema.previewHelp: Preview tables and fields available to this conversation.
schema.pasteHint: Copied table and field names can be pasted into the chat input.

welcome.title: Start with the workspace map
welcome.body: Left is where you configure resources, middle is where you ask the data question, and right is where you watch the task run and review outputs.

resources.title: Understand workspace resources
resources.body: The left panel covers Data Sources, Knowledge, Agent Tools / MCP, Skills, Models, and Assets / Files. These are the building blocks the agent can use for each task.

datasource.title: Confirm the datasource
datasource.bodyReady: A datasource is ready: {{id}}. This is what the agent will query for the first task.
datasource.bodyMissing: Add or enable a datasource before running the sample task. Open datasource config, create and test the connection, then return to this guide. The demo datasource is the fastest path when it is available.

model.bodyReady: A model profile is available. The model understands the request, helps generate SQL, and summarizes the result.
model.bodyMissing: Quick start needs an enabled LLM profile. Open model configuration, create and test a profile, then return to this guide.

send.title: Send the task
send.bodyReady: The task has started. The agent will inspect the datasource and run a read-only query.
send.bodyWaiting: Review the sample task in the chat input, then click the send arrow. This step unlocks after the run starts.

console.body: The Task Console shows run status, steps, tool calls, traces, and generated outputs while the task is running.

output.bodyFailed: The run failed. Check the model and datasource configuration, then try the sample task again.
output.bodyReady: When the run completes, review the answer and outputs here. You can ask a follow-up question or inspect the trace.

gallery.title: Choose a datasource type
gallery.help: Select one of the backend-enabled adapters. The next step renders the exact connection fields returned by the datasource type schema.
gallery.empty: No datasource types match your search.

local-files.title: Local files
local-files.description: Embedded, local, and uploaded tabular files.

relational.title: Relational databases
relational.description: Classic OLTP and enterprise SQL databases.

warehouse.title: Warehouses and lakehouse
warehouse.description: Analytical stores and cloud SQL warehouses.

explorer.noticeMongodb: MongoDB collections are exposed as table-like objects for simple readonly SELECT previews.
explorer.noticeRedis: Redis keyspace is exposed through a redis_keys pseudo table.
explorer.noticeSearch: Search indexes are exposed as table-like objects from mappings.
explorer.previewApiUnavailable: Datasource row preview API is not available yet.
explorer.loadSchemaHint: Load schema to browse tables, collections, indexes, or pseudo tables.
explorer.previewHint: Row preview uses the planned datasource table preview REST endpoint and respects sample/masking policy.
explorer.previewEmpty: Select a table and load a preview when the backend endpoint is available.

dataLink.title: Data Link
dataLink.subtitle: Datalink MCP workspace
dataLink.confirmRemoveTable: Remove this table from the data link?

datasourceId.label: Data source ID
datasourceId.helpText: Stable identifier forwarded to the Agent (forwardedProps.datasourceId).

type.label: Data source type
type.helpText: Implemented: DuckDB / SQLite / CSV / Excel / PostgreSQL / MySQL / ClickHouse. Disabled extension types are marked pending backend.

mode.label: Access mode
mode.helpText: The backend currently supports read-only queries only (run_sql_readonly).

filePath.label: File path
filePath.placeholder: Upload a local file, or paste an existing server path
filePath.helpText: Choose a local DuckDB / SQLite / CSV / Excel / Access file to upload. The server stores it under your workspace and fills this path automatically. You can still paste an existing server-readable path if you already have one.

connectionString.label: ODBC connection string
connectionString.helpText: Optional for Access. When set, overrides building the ODBC string from the file path.

password.label: Password
password.helpText: Stored in secretRef; read APIs never return plaintext.

tableAllowlist.label: Table allowlist
tableAllowlist.helpText: Comma-separated. Leave empty to allow all tables.

denyWrite.label: Deny writes
denyWrite.helpText: The backend currently enforces read-only access; this policy switch is pending.

maskFields.label: Masked fields
maskFields.helpText: Comma-separated field names to mask in query results.

scoreThreshold.label: Score threshold
scoreThreshold.helpText: Default filtering threshold for knowledge search and retrieve_knowledge.

embeddingApiKey.label: Embedding API Key
embeddingApiKey.helpText: Stored in secretRef. Falls back to server EMBEDDING_* environment variables when unset.

transport.label: Transport
transport.helpText: Remote MCP commonly uses SSE or Streamable HTTP; stdio uses a local launch command.

serverUrl.label: Endpoint / launch command
serverUrl.helpText: For remote transports, enter the MCP service URL. For stdio, a full launch command can be used as fallback.

apiUrl.label: Data API endpoint
apiUrl.helpText: Used by the DataLink workspace graph. Leave empty for MCP-only services.

command.label: Executable
command.helpText: In stdio mode, command + args are preferred. Leave empty to fall back to the full command above.

args.label: Launch arguments
args.helpText: Space-separated; written to the backend args array.

env.label: Environment variables (JSON)
env.helpText: JSON object with string keys and values.

apiKey.label: Token / API Key
apiKey.helpText: Stored in secretRef for Bearer authentication.

toolAllowlist.label: Tool allowlist
toolAllowlist.helpText: Comma-separated. Leave empty to allow all tools in the manifest.

provider.label: Provider
provider.helpText: All chat models use the OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions path.

apiKey.label: API Key
apiKey.helpText: Stored in secretRef; never sent through AG-UI during runs.

modelName.label: Model Name
modelName.helpText: Chat model id (for example gpt-4o, qwen-plus, deepseek-chat).

fallbackProfileId.label: Fallback Profile
fallbackProfileId.helpText: Fallback chain used when the primary profile fails.

timeoutMs.label: Timeout (ms)
timeoutMs.helpText: Whole-run timeout for multi-step agent tasks (not just a single LLM call). Use 300000–600000 for deep analysis.

defaultDbIds.label: Default data sources
defaultDbIds.helpText: Comma-separated datasource ids. Added automatically to the run when the skill matches.
